seoTitle: Backup and Restore Data in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: How to schedule automated backups and roll back your QuickBooks Online company file to a previous point in time.
intro: File corruption, user errors, or broken app syncs can put weeks of bookkeeping at risk. QuickBooks Online's Advanced backup utilities let you schedule automated version history and roll back your data to a specific point in time when something goes wrong. Here's how the backup and restore process works.

steps.heading: Open the backup settings
steps.body: Click the Gear icon and select Back up company to reach the Backup & Restore dashboard.

steps.heading: Create a manual backup
steps.body: Click Backup, then New Manual Backup to run an instant snapshot of your accounting records and attachments.

steps.heading: Confirm and save
steps.body: Select your data parameters and click Create to lock in that version.

steps.heading: Locate a restore point
steps.body: In the timeline grid, find the specific date and time stamp you want to roll back to.

steps.heading: Run the restoration
steps.body: Click Restore next to that version, review the pre-restore compliance checklist, type the confirmation phrase, and execute the restore.

faq.question: How do I know which backup version to restore?
faq.answer: The timeline grid shows exact date and time stamps for each saved version, so you can choose the one right before the issue occurred.

faq.question: Will restoring undo transactions added after that backup point?
faq.answer: Yes, restoring rolls your data back to the chosen point in time, so anything entered afterward gets overwritten.

faq.question: Is this backup feature available on every QuickBooks Online plan?
faq.answer: This capability is generally tied to the Advanced tier or specific Intuit app additions, so check your subscription level.

faq.question: How can I check what changed before deciding to restore?
faq.answer: Review the global Audit Log report first to see exactly what modifications were made before committing to a restore.
